goal: Implement enforceable AI quality thresholds by: (1) adding a `critical` field to eval cases, (2) wiring the existing title-quality and criteria-quality LLM judge prompts into `PromptEvalRunner` so draft-assist cases collect real judge scores, (3) committing the most recent eval output as a regression baseline and creating an `eval-thresholds.json` config, (4) building a threshold enforcement script that reads eval results and compares against the config + baseline, (5) wiring that script into the GitLab `eval:llm-judge` job, and (6) adding unit tests for the enforcement script. The result is a pipeline where deterministic checks hard-gate, judge scores soft-gate with clear pass/fail reporting, and regressions against baseline are detected. Constraints: backend-only for steps 1-3 (no frontend modifications); Java must pass spotlessCheck; preserve existing PromptEvalRunner parameterized test structure; judge scoring is optional (skip if no OPENROUTER_API_KEY); do NOT modify FaithfulnessEvaluator.java; eval results write to backend/build/eval-results/; enforcement script is standalone Node.js (scripts/eval-threshold-check.js) using only Node 20 built-ins.

plan.goal: Implement enforceable AI quality thresholds by: (1) adding a `critical` field to eval cases, (2) wiring the existing title-quality and criteria-quality LLM judge prompts into `PromptEvalRunner` so draft-assist cases collect real judge scores, (3) committing the most recent eval output as a regression baseline and creating an `eval-thresholds.json` config, (4) building a threshold enforcement script that reads eval results and compares against the config + baseline, (5) wiring that script into the GitLab `eval:llm-judge` job, and (6) adding unit tests for the enforcement script. The result is a pipeline where deterministic checks hard-gate, judge scores soft-gate with clear pass/fail reporting, and regressions against baseline are detected. Constraints: backend-only for steps 1-3 (no frontend modifications); Java must pass spotlessCheck; preserve existing PromptEvalRunner parameterized test structure; judge scoring is optional (skip if no OPENROUTER_API_KEY); do NOT modify FaithfulnessEvaluator.java; eval results write to backend/build/eval-results/; enforcement script is standalone Node.js (scripts/eval-threshold-check.js) using only Node 20 built-ins.

steps.title: Add `critical` field to EvalCase/EvalResult and mark critical cases in fixture JSON
steps.description:
Add an optional `critical` boolean field to `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/EvalCase.java` record. Jackson 2.18.3 defaults missing primitive record fields to `false`, so no extra annotation is needed beyond the existing `@JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)`.

Update `EvalResult.java` (a class, not a record, with a 5-arg constructor: `(String caseId, String description, String promptVersion, boolean schemaValid, String rawOutput)`) to add `boolean critical` as a 6th constructor parameter. Add a `isCritical()` getter. Include `critical` in the `toString()` output and ensure it is serialized in the JSON report (Jackson will pick up `isCritical()` automatically as a property).

Update all 6 `new EvalResult(...)` call sites in `PromptEvalRunner.java` (one per eval type: commitDraftAssist, commitLint, rcdoSuggest, riskSignal, reconcileAssist, ragQuery) to pass `evalCase.critical()` as the 6th argument.

Update fixture JSON files to mark critical cases:
- `backend/src/test/resources/eval/commit-draft-assist/cases.json` â€” add `"critical": true` to cases `draft-001`, `draft-004`, `draft-008`
- `backend/src/test/resources/eval/commit-lint/cases.json` â€” add `"critical": true` to cases `lint-001`, `lint-003`, `lint-004`

Scope: Only modify files in `backend/src/test/`. Do NOT modify any file in `backend/src/main/` or `frontend/`.

steps.title: Wire title-quality and criteria-quality LLM judges into PromptEvalRunner for draft-assist cases
steps.description:
Extend `PromptEvalRunner.evaluateCommitDraftAssist()` to call the title-quality judge and criteria-quality judge when the provider is available and the draft-assist output includes a non-null `suggestedTitle` or `suggestedSuccessCriteria`.

Critical implementation note: `OpenRouterAiProvider.generateSuggestion()` cannot be used for judge calls â€” it hard-codes its own system prompt and builds its own user message from `AiContext` fields. Since the constraint prohibits modifying `src/main/`, create a private helper method `callJudge(String filledPrompt)` in `PromptEvalRunner` that:
1. Reads `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and `OPENROUTER_MODEL` from env vars
2. Makes a direct HTTP POST to `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions` using `java.net.http.HttpClient`
3. Parses the response to extract the JSON content (strip markdown fences if present)
4. Returns the parsed `JsonNode` scores

Read judge prompt templates from test classpath resources:
- `eval/judge-prompts/title-quality-judge.txt`
- `eval/judge-prompts/criteria-quality-judge.txt`

Substitute placeholders (`{originalTitle}`, `{suggestedTitle}`, `{chessPiece}`, `{commitTitle}`, `{suggestedCriteria}`) and use the filled template as the user message.

Title judge dimensions stored via `addScore()`: `titleJudge_clarity`, `titleJudge_specificity`, `titleJudge_appropriate_scope`, `titleJudge_improvement`, `titleJudge_professional_tone`.

Criteria judge dimensions: `criteriaJudge_measurable`, `criteriaJudge_completeness`, `criteriaJudge_achievable_in_one_week`, `criteriaJudge_testable`.

Guard all judge calls with the existing `providerAvailable` boolean. Wrap in try/catch so judge failures never fail the test.

**Spotless formatting**: This step adds ~80-100 lines of new Java code (HTTP client, JSON parsing, template substitution). After editing, run `cd backend && ./gradlew spotlessApply` to auto-format before committing. The `build` check includes `spotlessCheck` and will fail if formatting doesn't match Eclipse JDT conventions.

Scope: Only modify `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/eval/PromptEvalRunner.java`. Do NOT modify any file in `backend/src/main/` or `frontend/`.

steps.title: Commit the most recent eval run as baseline and create eval-thresholds.json
steps.description:
Create two new files at the repo root:

**`eval-baseline.json`**: Copy the content from `backend/build/eval-results/eval-2026-03-26T16-33-06.574104Z.json` (the most recent real eval run) as the initial regression anchor. Important notes:
- This file is on local disk in a gitignored `build/` directory â€” read it from disk, not from git.
- This baseline predates Step 1 so results will NOT have a `critical` field â€” the enforcement script (Step 4) must handle missing `critical` gracefully.
- This baseline contains only 18 results (12 draft-assist + 6 lint), while fixture files define cases across 6 eval types. Future eval runs will produce more results. The enforcement script (Step 4) must handle differing population sizes: use per-case ID matching for `critical_failures_added` regression checks, not raw population counts.

**`eval-thresholds.json`**: A configuration file with this structure:
```json
{
  "deterministic": {
    "schema_valid_rate": 1.00,
    "overall_case_pass_rate": 0.95,
    "critical_case_pass_rate": 1.00
  },
  "judge_high_stakes": {
    "applies_to": ["RISK_SIGNAL", "TEAM_INSIGHT", "PERSONAL_INSIGHT", "RAG_QUERY"],
    "mean_faithfulness_min": 0.90,
    "critical_faithfulness_min": 0.85,
    "mean_relevancy_min": 0.80
  },
  "judge_assistive": {
    "applies_to": ["COMMIT_DRAFT_ASSIST", "COMMIT_LINT", "RCDO_SUGGEST", "RECONCILE_ASSIST"],
    "mean_faithfulness_min": 0.85,
    "mean_relevancy_min": 0.75,
    "title_judge_clarity_min": 0.70,
    "criteria_judge_measurable_min": 0.70
  },
  "regression": {
    "faithfulness_drop_max": 0.03,
    "pass_rate_drop_max": 0.05,
    "critical_failures_added_max": 0
  }
}
```

Scope: Only create files at the repo root. Do NOT modify any backend or frontend source files.

steps.title: Build the threshold enforcement script
steps.description:
Create `scripts/eval-threshold-check.js` â€” a standalone Node.js script (no external dependencies beyond Node 20 built-ins) that:

1. Reads `eval-thresholds.json` from the repo root (resolved relative to the script's location via `path.resolve(__dirname, '..')`)
2. Reads the latest `*.json` file from `backend/build/eval-results/` (sorted by filename timestamp, takes most recent)
3. Reads `eval-baseline.json` from the repo root (if it exists â€” skip regression checks when absent)
4. Computes: `schema_valid_rate`, `overall_case_pass_rate`, `critical_case_pass_rate` (skip if no results have `critical: true`), and mean of each judge score dimension
5. A result "passes" when `schemaValid === true` and all entries in `checks` are `true`
6. **Type classification for judge thresholds**: Eval results don't have an explicit type field. Infer the suggestion type from `promptVersion` using a prefix map: `commit-draft-assist-*` â†’ `COMMIT_DRAFT_ASSIST`, `commit-lint-*` â†’ `COMMIT_LINT`, `rcdo-suggest-*` â†’ `RCDO_SUGGEST`, `risk-signal-*` â†’ `RISK_SIGNAL`, `reconcile-assist-*` â†’ `RECONCILE_ASSIST`, `rag-query-*` â†’ `RAG_QUERY`. Match the inferred type against `judge_high_stakes.applies_to` and `judge_assistive.applies_to` arrays in the config to determine which thresholds apply. If `promptVersion` doesn't match any known prefix, skip judge thresholds for that result.
7. Compares against thresholds from `eval-thresholds.json` (deterministic thresholds apply globally; judge thresholds apply per-type group as described above)
8. Compares against `eval-baseline.json` for regression checks: `pass_rate_drop` compares overall pass rates, `critical_failures_added` uses per-case ID matching (find cases present in both baseline and current results by `caseId`, count those that passed in baseline but failed now AND are `critical: true` in current results). This per-case approach handles differing population sizes between baseline and current runs.
9. Prints a clear summary table to stdout with âś…/âťŚ indicators
10. Exits with code 0 if all checks pass, code 1 if any hard threshold is violated

The script must handle: missing judge scores (skip judge checks, note "judge scores unavailable"), missing `eval-baseline.json` (skip regression checks, warn), missing `critical` field on baseline results (treat as non-critical), missing eval results directory or empty results (exit with error).

Architecture: Extract core logic into exported functions (`computeMetrics`, `checkThresholds`, `checkRegression`) for unit testability. Use `if (require.main === module) { main(); }` guard pattern. Use CommonJS (`module.exports`) since root `package.json` does not have `"type": "module"`.

Scope: Only create `scripts/eval-threshold-check.js`. Do NOT modify any backend or frontend source files.

steps.title: Split CI into two jobs: eval run (manual/allow_failure) + threshold check (hard-gate), and add npm scripts
steps.description:
Update `.gitlab-ci.yml` to split the eval pipeline into two jobs so deterministic thresholds actually hard-gate:

**Job 1: `eval:llm-judge`** (modify existing) â€” Keep as `manual`, `allow_failure: true`, image `gradle:8.10.2-jdk21`. It runs `cd backend && ./gradlew --no-daemon evalTest` and produces eval result artifacts. No changes to its script, but ensure its artifacts include `eval-thresholds.json` and `eval-baseline.json` (or the threshold-check job checks them out from the repo root). Since artifacts already include `backend/build/eval-results/`, just keep that.

**Job 2: `eval:threshold-check`** (new job) â€” Add a NEW job in the `eval` stage that:
- Uses image `node:20-bookworm` (Node.js available natively, no install needed)
- Sets `needs: ["eval:llm-judge"]` so it runs only after the eval completes and receives its artifacts
- Does NOT set `allow_failure: true` (default is false) â€” so a threshold violation exit code 1 will hard-gate the pipeline
- Has `rules` matching `eval:llm-judge` (i.e., `if: '$OPENROUTER_API_KEY'` with `when: on_success`)
- Script: `node scripts/eval-threshold-check.js`
- The script resolves paths relative to `__dirname`/repo root, so `eval-thresholds.json` and `eval-baseline.json` are read from the checked-out repo, and `backend/build/eval-results/` comes from the `eval:llm-judge` artifacts

This separation means: the LLM call itself can flake without blocking (`allow_failure: true`), but once results exist, threshold violations are enforced (`allow_failure` defaults to `false` on the new job).

Add convenience npm scripts to `package.json`:
- `"eval:check": "node scripts/eval-threshold-check.js"`
- `"eval:run": "cd backend && ./gradlew evalTest"`

Scope: Only modify `.gitlab-ci.yml` and `package.json`. Do NOT modify any backend or frontend source files.

steps.title: Add unit tests for the enforcement script and verify end-to-end
steps.description:
Create `scripts/__tests__/eval-threshold-check.test.js` that tests the threshold check logic with synthetic eval results. The test must `require()` the exported functions from `scripts/eval-threshold-check.js` (see Step 4 architecture). Use Node 20's built-in test runner (`node:test` module) and `node:assert`. Use CommonJS since root package.json does not have `"type": "module"`.

Test cases:
- All passing â†’ no violations
- Schema validity failure â†’ violation reported
- Critical case failure â†’ violation reported
- Regression detected (pass rate drop) â†’ violation reported
- Missing judge scores â†’ skip judge checks, pass on deterministic only
- Missing baseline â†’ skip regression checks, warn

The test should be runnable via `node --test scripts/__tests__/eval-threshold-check.test.js`.

Update `package.json` root `test` script to also run the script tests so the `unit` check picks them up. The current `test` script is: `npm run test -w frontend -- --run && npm run test -w @weekly-commit/shared -- --run && cd backend && ./gradlew test`. **CRITICAL working directory bug to avoid**: the `cd backend && ./gradlew test` portion leaves the shell cwd inside `backend/`. You MUST `cd` back to the repo root before running the node tests. Append `&& cd .. && node --test scripts/__tests__/*.test.js` (NOT just `&& node --test ...` which would resolve the glob to `backend/scripts/__tests__/` which does not exist).

Scope: Create files in `scripts/__tests__/` and update `package.json`.

activeSummary.stepTitle: Add `critical` field to EvalCase/EvalResult and mark critical cases in fixture JSON
